title: indigofera howellii
intro: Indigofera howellii, also known as Howell's indigo, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States. It is a shrub-like plant with clusters of small purple flowers that has been used for its medicinal and therapeutic properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and infections. Indigofera howellii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves for topical application, as well as being used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
